TextENGNew: Whether this place was functioning as an actual school was genuinely questionable.
P.E. class, in particular, was a complete mess.
The only thing we did as a group was the warm-up stretches at the start.
After that, the teacher wasn't even there. Everyone just ran loose doing whatever they liked.
"It can't be helped. Everyone's a different age and gender."
"The only actual rule for P.E. is that we have to move our bodies while we're on the school grounds."
The smaller kids were running amok.
They were certainly moving their bodies. Probably getting plenty of exercise, but...
"...The Board of Education has definitely completely forgotten this school exists."
"...Sorry to keep you waiting."
"Now then, everyone! What shall we do for P.E. today?!"
"Alright. Now the whole usual crew is here."
"So, Class Rep—what shall we be doing for P.E. today? What shall we do?"
Mion harrumphed and crossed her arms with imperious authority, surveying the grounds.
"Explosive speed and stamina. In the world of sport, there are no friends. Everyone is a rival. The only thing you can rely on is your own body!"
"What is that even supposed to mean? You read way too much manga."
I retorted without missing a beat.
"And so—back to basics!! The tried-and-true king of all outdoor brawl sports, with every element packed inside: we're playing tag!"
That was a surprisingly precious choice for all the buildup.
"I was hoping you'd say that!!! Slowpoke Keiichi-san is obviously going to be 'it' first!"
"...I won't be beaten either."
"Rena will do her best! You're not beating me today!"
changeReason: New translation adds rena, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "...Why are all of you so fired up already...?"
"Article Three of the Club Charter!! You must participate in every game with your full and undivided enthusiasm!!!"
"So does that make this an official club activity?!"
Everyone exchanged a conspiring smirk.
Look at all this confidence. I'm a man.
Physically, I shouldn't be losing to any girl my own age.
And yet Mion and Satoko were fully expecting to win, while Rena was looking at me with something like pity.
"...Fine. Tag—I'm iiiiiiiiin!!!"
My battle cry echoed across the schoolyard.
The rules were as follows:
Anyone who evaded being tagged until the bell rang was a winner.
But whoever was 'it' didn't change—anyone who got tagged became 'it' too.
Which meant the number of people who were 'it' could multiply without limit.
The endgame would be a screaming, chaotic siege.
"Around here we call this version 'zombie tag,' you see."
"Makes sense. Anyone bitten by a zombie turns into one."
"...Why do you say it in such a creepy way... Creepy, isn't it?"
"Once I get Rena, I'm going to tear out her entrails while she's still alive...!!"
"D-D-Don't, Keiichi-kun, that's awful—!"
Rika-chan placed her hand gently on top of Rena's head as she flustered.
"...Don't worry. ...Before Keiichi can eat you, I'll eat you up gently."
"Rika, that isn't comforting at all."
Mion and I both nodded deeply in agreement.
"So, who's the first zombie? How do we decide? ...Rock-paper-scissors?"
"We're technically still in class time, so let's decide by answering a question. I'll ask, and whoever can't answer becomes the zombie!"
I had no idea how that was supposed to count as a class.
"What is '6' in English?!"
changeReason: New translation changes numerical values (old: set(), new: {'6'}).

TextENGNew: Huh? ...The sudden question threw me for a loop.
Mion repeated herself.
"So answer! What is '6' in English?!"
changeReason: New translation changes numerical values (old: set(), new: {'6'}).

TextENGNew: "Um, it's six!!"
"What about 'socks'?!"
"Socks!"
"Third letter from the end of the alphabet!"
"What is 'gender' in Japanese?!"
"Of course I know that!"
Satoko stopped herself just as she was about to answer, and swallowed the word.
...Ah, I see. Now that was a trap of a question.
"You're a worldly young lady, aren't you～? Surely you know what it is, right??"
"I-I certainly do...! Of course I know!!"
"Then go ahead and say it out loud. 'That thing,' you know? 'That.' What is 'that' in English?!"
"...Hau... Satoko-chan looks like she's struggling... how adorable..."
"Don't take her home. That's a crime."
I had to nip that in the bud before it became a real possibility.
Mion pressed Satoko relentlessly as she stammered.
"Come now, say it! Nice and loud!! What is 'that' in English?!?!"
"I do know!! I do!! I do!!! ...S-So, it's..."
"...That. 'That.'"
changeReason: Old translation gives a dictionary definition of 'that' while new simply says the word, matching JPN ザットですよ ('That,' as in 'that').

TextENGNew: "Huh? ...Oh."
We all blinked at Rika-chan's unexpected answer.
Right—'that' in English is just 'that.' ...We'd nearly walked right into that one.
...What would I have done if I'd been in Satoko's position?
...I'd probably have snapped and just screamed the word at the top of my lungs...
You'd make a terrifying enemy, Mion Sonozaki!
Good thing you were born a girl.
If you'd been a boy, you'd just be a creep...
"It... it seems I have no choice. ...This is most regrettable, but I, Satoko Houjou, shall assume the role of the zombie."
"...I shall devour every last one of you!!"
She seemed positively motivated.
"I just have to count to one hundred?!"
"Counting to one hundred invites cheating. Once you can answer my question, you may start running."
"Keiichi-kun, please make it a simple one..."
"One-fifth of a cake, one-sixth of a cake, and one-seventh of a cake are placed on a single plate."
"Ugh!! D-Different denominators?!?!"
Satoko grabbed a stick in a panic and started drawing fractions and a cake in the dirt.
"If Satoko, who can eat one whole cake in sixty seconds, eats all of them—how many cakes are left on the plate?"
The moment I finished, Mion shouted.
"Ready... go!!!!"
On Mion's signal, everyone scattered in every direction, leaving Satoko where she stood.
"Heheh... Keiichi-kun, that wasn't really a question at all, was it?"
Satoko had already lost the moment she started computing it.
She was eating it all, so of course there was nothing left on the plate!
Everyone dashed off toward their chosen hiding spots.
Knowing the terrain, they probably had their favorite escape routes already mapped out.
My disadvantage was immediately obvious.
At a moment like this, it would have been smarter to stick with someone who had survival instincts—someone like Mion.
...Not thinking of that at the start was going to cost me.
I glanced back at the schoolyard. Satoko had just risen to her feet and was getting moving.
She was furious. Heheh. ...Getting caught by a trick question that simple.
My current position was the corner of the school building.
I had long sightlines in two directions, which meant I could react quickly to any approaching zombies.
First, I caught my breath and switched into the competitive mindset club activities had been drilling into me.
...Think calmly, Keiichi Maebara. ...If I were 'it,' what would I do?
Growing your numbers is the fastest path to victory.
...So going after the weakest target first is the obvious play.
Meaning me!
"Now then—where could Keiichi-san be?! I won't let you escape!!!"
...Well, obviously.
But what's the best method to track me specifically?
Footprints, scent... some kind of trail.
If I could conceal my tracks cleverly enough... Satoko would have nothing to follow!
But this wasn't some crime thriller—could you actually pull off that kind of thing in a school game?
"Tomita~ Okamura~! Have you seen Keiichi-san anywhere?!"
WHAT is that?!?!
Modern zombies conduct interrogations to track their prey?!?!
Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun were pointing straight at my hiding spot.
The moment I confirmed Satoko was heading this way, I abandoned the position and fled.
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: Hiding completely with all these little kids running around wasn't easy.
My disadvantage from not knowing this terrain was becoming painfully clear...!
If that was how she was going to play it... I'd fight information warfare with information warfare...!!
I beckoned over some girls playing with a ball.
"...Sorry to ask, but could you pass on a message? Tell Satoko Houjou that her parents are waiting at the school gate."
"A message, a message, ahahahaha~! Sure sure!"
I put out a hand to stop them before they could run off.
"Wait—not yet! Also—take one to Mion Sonozaki too. Tell her the teacher is calling for her at the gate."
Heh... even I had to admire my own cunning.
If this worked, Satoko and Mion would run straight into each other at the gate!!
It would put me at a disadvantage to have more people become 'it,' but this was Mion—she'd find a way to slip out.
...And that was fine. As long as it bought me more time...!!
Ha ha ha—dance, Mion and Satoko!! In the palm of my haaaaand!!!
Having savored my moment of villainous scheming, I went looking for somewhere to hide.
Realistically, I'd bought myself only a small amount of time. ...And it might even come back to bite me...!
Once the information campaign was exposed, I'd obviously become the target of one of those same "messages."
Satoko would almost certainly ask the messengers to help her search for me.
That meant... there could be more zombies hunting me than there were actual game participants!!
The tiny little virus I'd released in a moment of mischief might be about to cause an epidemic...
Classmates turning one by one... all of them hunting only me!
...This plan... might actually have tightened the noose around my own neck.
I shuddered at the looming biohazard scenario and went looking for a hideout.
I found a storage shed next to the incinerator behind the school building.
I climbed up to the roof and lay flat, holding my breath.
Not a bad spot to hold out.
Good line of sight, and in an emergency I could jump down on any of three sides and run!
It was getting noisy below. The younger students were darting around under the shed.
"No sign of Maebara-san. Any luck over there?"
"Nope~. Where's Keiichi-san? His dad is at the front gate."
Definitely a lie.
Using the same word I had—'gate'—reeked of revenge. Mion was behind this...!
I was still one step ahead. Sorry about this, underclassmen—but you're going to be searching until the bell rings.
"Hey, does anyone know where Keiichi-san is? His mom suddenly got really sick!"
"Message for Maebara-san: 'Your house is on fire—get to the gate immediately!'"
"A jumbo jet has apparently crashed into the Maebara residence!"
"The police are here to question him!"
...Anything goes, apparently.
"Apparently his hobby is collecting body pillows."
WHAT?!
"Is it true he sleeps hugging them every night?"
Who are we even talking about?!
"I heard he can't get enough of how soft and squishy they feel!"
There's absolutely no way that's true!!
"I heard one of them even has a photo of the Class Rep on it!"
"WHAAAT!?"
GRRAAAAAAAARRGHH!!!!!
This is your handiwork, MIONNNN!!!
C-Calm down, Keiichi Maebara!! This is Mion's tactic to flush me out! Hold it together!!
If my underclassmen stopped and thought rationally, they'd realize it's all garbage!
...Small children, however, do not stop and think rationally.
To them every one of those "facts" was gospel, and they chortled away while fanning out to search for me.

Heheheh...
Bwahahaha...!
I win, Mion!
Victory is mine!
Bwahahaha...!!
Wiping unstoppable tears from my eyes, I reveled in my humble triumph...
"Did you hear? Apparently the new transfer student Maebara-san is totally into anime girls! Kyahahahaha!!"
The price of outsmarting Mion was turning out to be rather high...
Oh! ...Someone's down below.
That's... Rena and Rika-chan.
"...Haah haah. Rika-chan, you're still in the clear?!"
"...Somehow holding on."
"...Haah haah. ...It looks like Mii-chan's been turned too..."
Mion?! ...Could it be—she got caught because of my little scheme?!
That explained the relentless message counterattack. ...But if so, this wasn't good...
changeReason: New translation removes mion, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "We managed to shake Mii-chan, but Satoko-chan... she..."
"...Satoko is searching around the pipes... so we should be safe here for a while."
Those words let me breathe a little easier too.
While Rena slumped down to catch her breath, Rika-chan was slowly creeping up on her...
Rika-chan moving silently was nothing new, but something felt off. ...It couldn't be—
"Wha?! ...Rika-chan? What is it? What is it?! ...Haoh...?!"
"...Don't be scared."
I had never seen Rika-chan's smile look quite so unsettling before.
"Wh-Why are you coming closer? Why?! Rika-chan's not... a zombie, is she?!"
"...There's nothing to fear. ...I'll eat you gently."
"N-No, that can't—Rika-chan, you wouldn't—Eeeek...!!"
Rena pressed her back against the wall as Rika-chan lurched toward her with both arms outstretched, every bit the zombie...
Rena trembled, back flat against the wall.
This was genuinely terrifying...
Like something straight out of a real zombie film.
At that moment, Rena's eyes found mine.
"K-Keiichi-kun!! Save meeee!!!"
Zombie Rika-chan rotated 180 degrees Exorcist-style and fixed her eyes on me!
"There! I found Kei-chan!!!!"
Mion had materialized on top of the cinderblock wall around the garbage corner and spotted me too.
My position was only good against one zombie. Being surrounded was another matter entirely!!!
"You found Keiichi-san~~?!?!"
Satoko's voice came from somewhere in the distance, and she was closing fast.
It looked like Rika-chan had lost Rena, and now had me squarely in her sights.
"Uuurgh~... oooough~..."
"...Coooome on dooown... Kei-chaaaaaan..."
All three zombies circled the shed, moaning curses at me.
"You—all of you are terrifying!! WAY too terrifying!!!"
"I wonder what Keiichi-san's insides taste like~?? Keiichi-saaaan...!"
"...Keiichi... I want you to join us."
"S-S-Somebody, please save meeeee!!!!!"
The one person who might have saved me—Rena—was visible across the schoolyard, apologizing with her whole body.
An apology. Sorry. Leaving me for dead? ...R-RENAAAAA!!!!!
I leapt off the roof in a panic—
—and Satoko and Rika-chan tackled me to the ground and proceeded to tickle me thoroughly.
"GWAHAHAHAHA!! Stop—STOP!! GWAAAAH!!!!!"
"...So that leaves Rena. There isn't much time until the bell."
"...Damn you, Renaaaa...!! How dare you... leave me for deaaaad!!"
So this is how zombies feel. Now I truly understood the anguish of a vengeful ghost.
"...It's so sad, so sad. ...But now Keiichi is one of us."
So this was what it felt like to be bitten by a vampire and turned into one of their own.
There was something strange about being welcomed by the very side that had been hunting you.
"...Zombie tag really does have layers to it!"
"This is no time to be waxing philosophical!! We must capture Rena!!!"
"The principal's walking down the hallway. ...Bell's going to ring soon!!"
Now that I was a creature of vengeance, I had to pursue every option to devour Rena.
But I was no ordinary zombie now. I was a vampire—lord of the undead!!
Using the same trick that had brought me low, I pressed my classmates into the service of evil.
I gathered up the younger students running around the schoolyard.
"Everyone, listen up! Rena is in danger! Find Rena immediately!"
"...More dangerous than a jumbo jet crashing into your family's house?"
...Oh right, that was the story now, wasn't it. Mion was whistling innocently, pretending to know nothing.
"Yeah! Jumbo jets don't even compare! What fell on Rena's house was—a comet!!"
"A-A COMET?!"

"That's right—aliens used a device to override the Roche limit and launched one to start. Even I didn't think they'd go this far. And now they're following up with one hundred more!! Earth's defenses were completely caught off guard!"
changeReason: New translation changes numerical values (old: {'100'}, new: set()).

TextENGNew: The younger students stared wide-eyed and dumbstruck at the scale of it all...
"An Earth F-Federation?! Are we actually at war with aliens?!"
"We are!! You know how Halley's Comet is coming back in three years? Aliens have been hiding inside it, and they've been plotting to invade Earth!"
The younger students all swallowed hard...!

"If things go on like this, the Earth is finished!! But there is hope! Only Rena and her supersonic Mach-Rena Punch can intercept all of the asteroids!! That's right—the Earth Army's supreme ace... is Rena Ryuugu!!"
"A-Amazing...!"
"So older kids really ARE different from us!"
They seemed to fully grasp that the fate of the Earth rested entirely on Rena's shoulders.
"Now scatter, comrades!! Find Rena!!!"
My junior comrades let out a resounding "Yeah!!" and sprinted off in every direction across the school grounds.
...Hm? One hadn't left.
"But... even someone like Rena-san couldn't push the moon back, could she...?"
"No need to worry! Just days ago, Rena deflected a comet bomb that was on a collision course with Earth. One or two hundred asteroids are nothing against a light-speed punch!!"
"...Ryuugu-san is incredible..."
Satisfied at last, she too sprinted off in search of Rena.
...You're all going to grow up to be fine adults someday.
changeReason: Old translation speaks about 'promising up-and-comers' (observer perspective) while new directly addresses characters ('You're all going to grow up to be fine adults'), matching JPN みんな、立派な大人になるんだぞ.

TextENGNew: "...This old man has seen a whole new side of Kei-chan today..."
That didn't quite feel like a compliment, but never mind.
"...Even Keiichi-san, though, you really came through...!! This is rather reassuring!!"
"With this many people... we win!! We absolutely win!!"
"...I think Keiichi should have just volunteered to be the zombie from the start."
Rika-chan's words stung a little, but I'd let it go for now...
Even Rena couldn't outrun the entire class.
After a thorough search by everyone, Rena was finally cornered in the back of the sports storage shed.
"Y-You're all scary!! Even Keiichi-kun...!! You're all so scary!!!"
Being hunted down by the whole school was probably genuinely frightening.
The younger students closed in on her, insisting she was the only one who could save the planet.
"I don't know what you're all talking about! Rena can't destroy asteroids just by punching them!"
changeReason: New translation adds rena, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "Renaaa, how dare you abandon me out there!! You'd better be ready to face the consequences...!?!?"
"I-I-I'm so sorry, Keiichi-kun... b-b-but I didn't have any choice...!!"
"Do not resist... Let yourself be devoured~...!!!"
"...Let us all... be together."
"Now, Rena... say your prayers!! Mwahahahaha!!!!!"
Rena had stumbled over a mat and huddled trembling in the back of the shed, on the verge of tears. I closed in.
A particular and rather immoral scenario came to mind unbidden, and my heart rate quickened just a fraction.
...Satoko and Rika-chan were both wiggling their fingers at her sides. They were obviously planning to tickle her.
Rena knew it too, and went rigid with the specific dread of knowing what was coming.
"...K-Keiichi-kun... you wouldn't do anything awful like everyone else... would you? ...Would you?"
"...Hmm, I wooonder~?!?!"
"...Now then... have you made your peace with what's coming?!"
"...I don't mind... as long as it's Keiichi-kun..."
The resigned smile on her face as she made her peace with it—it made my heart skip a beat.
"I-I believe... that Keiichi-kun won't do anything horrible to me..."
...Urgh... body won't... move... Was that some kind of anti-zombie monk's prayer?
The rational part of me rang the bell and the beast was put down in an instant.
And right then—the school bell!!  Game over.
"Yaaaaaay!! I did it, I did it!! I survived!! Yaaay~ yaaaaaay~☆!!"
Rena sprang to her feet and celebrated like the curse had been lifted.
"...Well then. Sunlight broke through at the last moment and the evil zombie horde crumbled to dust... The heroine surviving is... just how it goes in the movies, I suppose..."
"What are you going on about?! This is all your fault for dawdling, Keiichi-san!!"
"...Punishment is required."
Satoko and Rika-chan pinned me down and sentenced me, once again, to death by tickling.
"EEEeeeEEeek! F-Forgive me! EEEeeeEEeek!"
"So the award for surviving goes to Rena Ryuugu and Mion Sonozaki. Yay!"
"Huh...? Mii-chan, you weren't a zombie...?"
"Only pretending~ You have to fool your own allies before you can fool the enemy, you know?"
"...UUuUuuuuUURRGH!! MION—you little—!!"
"Yeah, yeah. Hurry up and change, okay~? Don't be late for the next class~!"
"Let go of me, Satoko! Rika-chan!! GwaaAaaAaa...!!!!"
Having been terrified down to my very marrow by yet another club activity, I swore solemnly to make Mion beg for mercy next time.
